layout.description: Direction in which panels are laid out. 'horizontal' places panels side by side; 'vertical' stacks them.

gutterSize.description: Width (or height) in pixels of the drag handle bar between panels.

onResizeEnd.description: Called after the user finishes a drag with the new first-panel size in pixels.

responsive.description: Container width in px (measured on the splitter itself, not the viewport) below which a horizontal splitter collapses from side-by-side panels into a stacked layout. Applies to layout="horizontal" only — a vertical splitter is already stacked and ignores it. The collapse only flips layout styles and swaps the draggable gutter for a static divider, so panels are never unmounted and child state is preserved. Omit to disable.

collapsedLayout.description: Direction panels stack in once collapsed (only used with responsive).

onCollapseChange.description: Called when the splitter crosses the responsive threshold, with the new collapsed state.

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the outer container.

style.description: Inline styles for the outer container.

children.description: Exactly two SplitterPanel children are required.

defaultSize.description: Initial size of the panel. Accepts px (e.g. '300px') or % (e.g. '40%'). Only one panel should set this — the sibling fills the remaining space.

minSize.description: Minimum size of the panel. Accepts px or %. Prevents the panel from being resized below this threshold.

fixed.description: When true, the panel cannot be resized by the user.

className.description: Additional CSS classes for the panel container.

style.description: Inline styles for the panel container.

children.description: Content to render inside the panel.
